column3: Test Name
column4: Action
column5: Description

column3: sRGB color checker patches match expected RGB values
column4: Samples pixels at patch coordinates and computes CIE76 Delta-E against expected sRGB values (threshold ≤ 5)
column5: Reads the sRGB color checker image and its companion JSON file containing known patch coordinates and expected RGB values. Samples the actual pixel color at each coordinate using sharp, then computes the CIE76 Delta-E perceptual color difference between expected and actual. Fails if any patch exceeds a Delta-E of 5, which would indicate a visible color shift.

column3: Adobe RGB color checker patches within Delta-E of sRGB targets
column4: Same patch sampling with wider gamut tolerance (threshold ≤ 25) for Adobe RGB color space conversion
column5: Reads the Adobe RGB color checker image (tagged with an Adobe RGB ICC profile) and its JSON file listing sRGB-converted target values. Samples pixels at each patch coordinate and computes Delta-E against the sRGB targets. Uses a wider threshold of 25 to account for gamut mapping differences when converting from Adobe RGB to sRGB.

column3: ProPhoto RGB color checker patches within Delta-E of sRGB targets
column4: Same patch sampling with wider gamut tolerance (threshold ≤ 25) for ProPhoto RGB gamut
column5: Same approach as CL-02 but for ProPhoto RGB, the widest gamut commonly used by cameras. Reads the ProPhoto-tagged image and its JSON targets, samples pixels, and checks Delta-E ≤ 25. ProPhoto has a much larger gamut than sRGB so conversion errors are expected to be larger than Adobe RGB.

column3: Custom ICC profile image has embedded ICC profile
column4: Checks that sharp detects an ICC profile in the image metadata
column5: Opens the custom ICC profile image with sharp and inspects its metadata to confirm an ICC profile buffer is present. This validates that the test fixture was correctly prepared with an embedded profile, which is a prerequisite for testing whether SmugMug preserves or converts custom profiles.

column3: Untagged image has no embedded ICC profile
column4: Confirms no ICC profile is present in the untagged fixture
column5: Opens the untagged image with sharp and confirms the ICC metadata field is falsy (null or undefined). This is the negative control for CL-04 — it ensures the fixture genuinely has no profile, so any test comparing tagged vs untagged behavior has a clean baseline.

column3: CMYK image is detected as CMYK color space
column4: Verifies metadata.space === cmyk
column5: Opens the CMYK test image with sharp and checks that metadata.space reports 'cmyk' and channels is 4. This confirms the fixture is a genuine CMYK file rather than an RGB file with a misleading name, which would invalidate any downstream CMYK conversion tests.

column3: 16-bit gradient has smooth tonal transitions
column4: Measures mean adjacent pixel difference along the middle row (must be < 5)
column5: Reads the 16-bit TIFF gradient image, converts it to 8-bit greyscale via sharp, then walks across the middle row measuring the absolute difference between each adjacent pixel pair. Computes the mean of all those differences. A smooth gradient should have a very low mean (< 5). High values indicate banding from bit-depth reduction or compression.

column3: Black and white point pixels match expected values
column4: Samples black/white/midgray patches at known coordinates (Delta-E ≤ 5)
column5: Reads the black/white test image and its JSON file specifying the exact coordinates of pure black, pure white, and mid-gray patches. Samples the actual RGB at each coordinate and computes Delta-E against the expected values. Fails if any patch exceeds Delta-E 5, catching level shifts where blacks get lifted or whites get crushed.

column3: Grayscale ramp steps match expected luminance values
column4: Samples 11 grayscale steps and compares measured luminance to expected values (tolerance ≤ 15)
column5: Reads the 11-step grayscale ramp image and its JSON file listing the expected luminance (0–255) at each step's coordinate. Samples the actual pixel, computes weighted luminance (0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B), and compares against the expected value. Fails if any step differs by more than 15, which would indicate gamma curve errors or tone mapping issues.

column3: Saturated color patches match expected RGB values
column4: Samples R/G/B/C/M/Y patches (Delta-E ≤ 5)
column5: Reads the saturated patches image containing solid Red, Green, Blue, Cyan, Magenta, and Yellow blocks. Uses the companion JSON to locate each patch's center coordinate and expected RGB. Samples actual pixels and computes Delta-E. Fails if any patch exceeds 5, catching desaturation or hue rotation in the most visually obvious colors.

column3: Candidate image is readable and has valid dimensions
column4: Verifies width and height > 0
column5: Opens quality-detail.jpg with sharp and reads its metadata. Verifies both width and height are greater than zero. This is a fast smoke test that catches corrupted files, truncated downloads, or images with malformed headers before running more expensive quality checks.

column3: Candidate SSIM vs baseline meets quality threshold
column4: Downsamples both to 1600px wide and computes SSIM (must be ≥ 0.92)
column5: Loads both the lossless PNG baseline and the JPEG candidate, downsamples each to 1600px wide using sharp (to keep the comparison fast on large images), converts to greyscale, then computes the Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) between them. SSIM ranges from 0 to 1 where 1 is identical. Fails if below 0.92, indicating visible structural degradation.

column3: Candidate file size is non-trivial
column4: File size > 10KB
column5: Reads quality-detail.jpg from disk and checks that the buffer length exceeds 10,000 bytes. Catches empty files, truncated writes, or placeholder stubs that might pass dimension checks but contain no real image data.

column3: Baseline file size is non-trivial
column4: File size > 10KB
column5: Same check as IQ-03 but for quality-reference.png. Ensures the lossless reference image itself isn't corrupted or empty, since it's used as the ground truth for SSIM and other comparisons.

column3: Candidate is a valid JPEG
column4: First 3 bytes are FF D8 FF
column5: Reads the first 3 bytes of quality-detail.jpg and verifies they are 0xFF 0xD8 0xFF, the JPEG magic number. Catches files that have a .jpg extension but aren't actually JPEG format (e.g., a renamed PNG or a file with a corrupted header).

column3: Baseline is a valid PNG
column4: First 4 bytes are 89 50 4E 47
column5: Reads the first 4 bytes of quality-reference.png and verifies they are 0x89 0x50 0x4E 0x47 (the PNG signature). Ensures the lossless baseline is genuinely PNG format, not a misnamed JPEG or corrupted file.

column3: Candidate image sharpness meets minimum threshold
column4: Laplacian variance > 50 (downsampled to 1600px)
column5: Downsamples quality-detail.jpg to 1600px wide, converts to greyscale, then applies a 3x3 Laplacian edge-detection kernel to every interior pixel. Computes the variance of the Laplacian responses. Sharp images with fine detail produce high variance (> 50). Low variance indicates the image has been over-smoothed or had sharpening stripped.

column3: quality-noisy.jpg has measurable sharpness variance
column4: Laplacian variance > 50
column5: Same Laplacian variance measurement as IQ-07 but on the high-ISO noisy image. Verifies the fixture has enough texture and grain to produce meaningful variance (> 50). A flat or blank image would break sharpness comparison tests that depend on this fixture.

column3: Candidate and baseline have matching aspect ratio
column4: Aspect ratio difference ≤ 0.05
column5: Reads metadata for both quality-reference.png and quality-detail.jpg, computes width/height ratio for each, and checks the absolute difference is ≤ 0.05. Catches unexpected cropping or padding that might have been introduced during format conversion or resizing of the test fixtures.

column3: Image renders and is visible at Ti 100x75
column4: Loads image in browser at 100x75 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Opens a new browser page at 100x75 viewport, injects the test image as a base64 data URL into an HTML page, waits for the image element to report complete with naturalWidth > 0. Verifies the browser can decode and display the image at the smallest SmugMug size tier.

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at Ti 100x75
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same setup as the render test, then takes a screenshot of the image element and compares it byte-for-byte against a saved baseline PNG in tests/baselines/. On first run (no baseline exists), saves the screenshot as the new baseline. Detects any pixel-level change in how the browser renders the image at this viewport size.

column3: Image renders and is visible at Th 150x112
column4: Loads image in browser at 150x112 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 150x112 viewport (Thumbnail tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at Th 150x112
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 150x112 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at S 400x300
column4: Loads image in browser at 400x300 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 400x300 viewport (Small tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at S 400x300
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 400x300 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at M 600x450
column4: Loads image in browser at 600x450 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 600x450 viewport (Medium tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at M 600x450
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 600x450 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at L 800x600
column4: Loads image in browser at 800x600 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 800x600 viewport (Large tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at L 800x600
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 800x600 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at XL 1024x768
column4: Loads image in browser at 1024x768 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 1024x768 viewport (XLarge tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at XL 1024x768
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 1024x768 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at X2 1280x960
column4: Loads image in browser at 1280x960 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 1280x960 viewport (X2Large tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at X2 1280x960
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 1280x960 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at X3 1600x1200
column4: Loads image in browser at 1600x1200 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 1600x1200 viewport (X3Large tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at X3 1600x1200
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 1600x1200 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at X4 1920x1440
column4: Loads image in browser at 1920x1440 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 1920x1440 viewport (X4Large tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at X4 1920x1440
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 1920x1440 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at X5 2048x1536
column4: Loads image in browser at 2048x1536 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 2048x1536 viewport (X5Large tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at X5 2048x1536
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 2048x1536 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at 4K 3840x2160
column4: Loads image in browser at 3840x2160 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 3840x2160 viewport (4K tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at 4K 3840x2160
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 3840x2160 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at 5K 5120x2880
column4: Loads image in browser at 5120x2880 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 5120x2880 viewport (5K tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at 5K 5120x2880
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 5120x2880 viewport

column3: Image renders and is visible at O 1280x720
column4: Loads image in browser at 1280x720 viewport and verifies naturalWidth > 0
column5: Same as IR-Ti-render but at 1280x720 viewport (Original tier)

column3: Screenshot matches baseline at O 1280x720
column4: Compares screenshot against saved baseline PNG
column5: Same as IR-Ti-baseline but at 1280x720 viewport

column3: Landscape image has correct dimensions
column4: Expected 6000x4000
column5: Opens c-sizing-landscape.jpg with sharp and reads width and height from metadata. Asserts width is exactly 6000 and height is exactly 4000. Validates the canonical landscape fixture has the precise dimensions that other sizing and render tests depend on.

column3: Portrait image has correct dimensions
column4: Expected 4000x6000
column5: Opens c-sizing-portrait.jpg with sharp and asserts width is 4000 and height is 6000. Ensures the portrait fixture wasn't accidentally rotated, cropped, or resized, which would invalidate portrait-specific sizing tests.

column3: Square image has equal width and height
column4: Expected 5000x5000
column5: Opens c-sizing-square.jpg with sharp and asserts both width and height are 5000. Verifies the square fixture is genuinely square, since 1:1 aspect ratios are an edge case that can expose rounding bugs in resize logic.

column3: Panoramic image has extreme landscape aspect ratio
column4: Expected 12000x2000, ratio > 5
column5: Opens c-sizing-panoramic.jpg with sharp and asserts width is 12000, height is 2000, and the width/height ratio exceeds 5. Validates the extreme landscape fixture that tests whether thumbnail generation and crop logic handle very wide images without padding or cropping.

column3: Tall image has extreme portrait aspect ratio
column4: Expected 2000x10000, ratio > 4
column5: Opens c-sizing-tall.jpg with sharp and asserts width is 2000, height is 10000, and the height/width ratio exceeds 4. Validates the extreme portrait fixture for testing height-dominant layout calculations and overflow handling.

column3: Small image has correct dimensions
column4: Expected 400x300
column5: Opens c-sizing-small.jpg with sharp and asserts width is 400 and height is 300. This fixture is used for browser data URL tests and upscaling checks, so its exact dimensions must be known and stable.

column3: Small image renders at correct natural dimensions in browser
column4: Browser naturalWidth/naturalHeight matches buffer dimensions
column5: Reads c-sizing-small.jpg dimensions from sharp, then loads it as a base64 data URL in a Playwright browser page. Waits for the image to fully load, then reads naturalWidth and naturalHeight from the DOM. Asserts both match the sharp-reported dimensions exactly, catching browser decoding bugs where reported size diverges from actual pixel data.

column3: All orientation images have valid EXIF orientation tag
column4: Orientation tag is numeric 1-8 using translateValues: false
column5: Parses each of the 16 orientation test images (8 landscape + 8 portrait) using exifr with translateValues: false to get the raw numeric orientation tag. Asserts each tag is between 1 and 8 inclusive. Catches images where the EXIF orientation tag was stripped or corrupted during file preparation.

column3: Each orientation image matches its expected tag value
column4: Each image's tag matches its expected value 1 through 8
column5: Same parsing as OR-01 but asserts each image's orientation tag matches the specific expected value (e.g., Landscape_3 should have tag 3, Portrait_6 should have tag 6). Catches images where the tag is present but has the wrong value, such as a 90-degree image incorrectly tagged as 180.

column3: All orientation images have valid non-zero dimensions
column4: Width and height > 0
column5: Opens each of the 16 orientation images with sharp and asserts width > 0 and height > 0. A bulk integrity check that catches format corruption specific to certain orientation tags — some JPEG encoders produce invalid files for uncommon orientations like 5 or 7.

column3: Landscape_1-Normal.jpg has landscape aspect ratio
column4: width > height
column5: Opens Landscape_1-Normal.jpg with sharp and asserts width > height. This is the ground truth check — orientation tag 1 means no rotation needed, so the raw pixel dimensions should already be landscape (wider than tall).

column3: Portrait_1-Normal.jpg has portrait aspect ratio
column4: height > width
column5: Opens Portrait_1-Normal.jpg with sharp and asserts height > width. Same ground truth check for the portrait series — raw pixels should already be taller than wide with no rotation needed.

column3: Landscape_6 (6000x4000 variant) has expected dimensions
column4: Expected 6000x4000
column5: Opens the high-resolution 6000x4000 orientation-6 variant with sharp and asserts width is 6000 and height is 4000. This dedicated large-format image has genuinely different raw pixel dimensions from the standard 600x450 orientation set, making it suitable for testing dimension-swap behavior in orientation-aware display logic.

column3: Landscape_3 (180°) has same dimensions as Landscape_1
column4: Identical width and height
column5: Opens both Landscape_1-Normal.jpg and Landscape_3-Rotated-180.jpg with sharp and asserts they have identical width and height. A 180-degree rotation should never change dimensions — this catches bugs where rotation logic incorrectly swaps width and height for non-90-degree rotations.

column3: Orientation reference images are valid
column4: Both have positive dimensions
column5: Opens both Landscape_orientation-reference.jpg and Portrait-orientation-reference.jpg with sharp and asserts each has width > 0 and height > 0. Validates the visual reference images (used for manual side-by-side comparison) are intact and readable.

column3: Camera make is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: Make field is truthy
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr and asserts the Make field is truthy (not undefined, null, or empty string). Make identifies the camera manufacturer (e.g., Canon, Nikon) and is one of the most commonly displayed metadata fields in photo info panels.

column3: Camera model is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: Model field is truthy
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr and asserts the Model field is truthy. Model identifies the specific camera body (e.g., EOS R5, Z9) and is displayed alongside Make in photo info panels.

column3: Exposure time is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: ExposureTime defined and > 0
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr and asserts ExposureTime is defined and greater than 0. ExposureTime (shutter speed) is a core shooting parameter that photographers rely on seeing in image details.

column3: Aperture (FNumber) is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: FNumber defined and > 0
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr and asserts FNumber is defined and greater than 0. FNumber (aperture) is part of the exposure triangle and is critical for photographers evaluating depth of field.

column3: ISO is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: ISO or ISOSpeedRatings present
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr looking for both ISO and ISOSpeedRatings tags, and asserts at least one is present. Cameras inconsistently use these two tag names for the same value, so both must be checked.

column3: Focal length is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: FocalLength defined and > 0
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr and asserts FocalLength is defined and greater than 0. Focal length is essential for photographers comparing lens performance and is commonly displayed in photo info panels.

column3: DateTimeOriginal is present in metadata-rich.jpg
column4: Valid parseable date
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr, extracts DateTimeOriginal, converts it to a JavaScript Date, and asserts it's a valid parseable date (not NaN). This is the most important metadata field for photo organization — it drives timeline views, sorting, and date-based search.

column3: metadata-stripped.jpg has no camera EXIF fields
column4: Make, Model, ISO, FNumber, ExposureTime all absent
column5: Parses metadata-stripped.jpg with exifr looking specifically for Make, Model, ISO, FNumber, and ExposureTime. Asserts none of these fields are present. This is the negative control — it confirms the stripped fixture is genuinely stripped so that 'missing metadata' assertions in other tests are meaningful.

column3: metadata-iptc.jpg has IPTC metadata present
column4: Parseable data with > 0 fields
column5: Parses metadata-iptc.jpg with exifr using iptc: true and all: true options, then asserts the result is non-null and contains more than zero fields. Validates the IPTC fixture actually has embedded caption/keyword data that downstream IPTC-specific tests depend on.

column3: All orientation test images are valid and readable
column4: Valid JPEG magic bytes, positive dimensions
column5: Scans TEST_IMAGES_DIR for files matching Landscape_{1-8} and Portrait_{1-8} patterns. For each file, checks the first two bytes are 0xFF 0xD8 (JPEG magic number), then opens with sharp and asserts width > 0 and height > 0. A bulk integrity check that catches corrupted orientation fixtures before they cause confusing failures in OR tests.

column3: metadata-rich.jpg EXIF contains required fields
column4: Make, Model, ExposureTime, FNumber, FocalLength present
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr using all: true, then checks for the presence of Make, Model, ExposureTime, FNumber, and FocalLength. Collects any missing fields and fails if any are absent. This validates the primary metadata fixture has the complete set of camera fields that all downstream metadata tests depend on.

column3: metadata-rich.jpg image format is JPEG
column4: format === jpeg
column5: Opens metadata-rich.jpg with sharp and reads the format field from metadata. Asserts it equals 'jpeg'. Catches format misidentification where a file has a .jpg extension but sharp detects a different format, which would indicate file corruption.

column3: metadata-rich.jpg EXIF DateTimeOriginal is a valid date
column4: Parseable date, year > 1990
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr extracting only DateTimeOriginal. If present, converts to a JavaScript Date and asserts it's valid (not NaN) and the year is greater than 1990. Catches malformed date strings that would break date-based sorting or display.

column3: Candidate image loads in browser without console errors
column4: No console errors or page errors during load
column5: Attaches listeners for console errors and page errors, then loads metadata-iptc.jpg as a base64 data URL in a Playwright browser page. Waits for the image to fully load (complete === true and naturalWidth > 0). Asserts zero errors were captured. Catches JavaScript decode errors, CORS issues, or malformed data URLs.

column3: Candidate image src attribute is set correctly
column4: src contains data:image
column5: Loads metadata-iptc.jpg as a data URL in a browser page, waits for full load, then reads the src attribute from the img element. Asserts it's truthy and contains the string 'data:image'. Validates the data URL was correctly constructed — a malformed src would show a broken image icon.

column3: Candidate naturalWidth in browser matches buffer width
column4: Browser naturalWidth === sharp metadata width
column5: Reads metadata-iptc.jpg width from sharp metadata, then loads the same image as a data URL in a browser page. Reads naturalWidth from the DOM img element and asserts it exactly matches the sharp-reported width. Catches browser decoding discrepancies where the rendered size doesn't match the file's actual pixel dimensions.

column3: Candidate naturalHeight in browser matches buffer height
column4: Browser naturalHeight === sharp metadata height
column5: Same approach as MD-03 but for height. Reads sharp metadata height, loads in browser, reads naturalHeight from DOM, asserts exact match. Important for layout calculations that depend on accurate natural dimensions.

column3: metadata-rich.jpg EXIF Make and Model are non-empty
column4: Non-empty trimmed strings
column5: Parses metadata-rich.jpg with exifr extracting Make and Model. For each field that exists, asserts it's a string type and that trimming whitespace leaves a non-empty result. Catches cases where fields exist in EXIF but contain empty strings or only whitespace, which would show blank in a UI info panel.

column3: metadata-stripped.jpg loads cleanly in browser
column4: Loads with naturalWidth > 0, no page errors
column5: Attaches a page error listener, loads metadata-stripped.jpg as a data URL in a browser page, waits for full load, then asserts naturalWidth > 0 and zero page errors. Ensures images with completely stripped metadata don't cause browser decode errors — the viewer must handle missing EXIF gracefully.

column3: POI test image loads and has valid dimensions
column4: Width and height > 0
column5: Opens c-poi-test.jpg with sharp and asserts width > 0 and height > 0. A smoke test ensuring the POI fixture is readable before running more expensive brightness centroid calculations.

column3: POI image brightness centroid is within image bounds
column4: Centroid (cx, cy) both between 0 and 1
column5: Downsamples c-poi-test.jpg to 100x100 greyscale, then computes the brightness-weighted centroid by summing x*luminance and y*luminance across all pixels. Normalizes to 0–1 range. Asserts both cx and cy are between 0 and 1 exclusive. Validates the centroid algorithm produces sane coordinates.

column3: Reference image brightness centroid is within image bounds
column4: Centroid (cx, cy) both between 0 and 1
column5: Same brightness centroid calculation as POI-02 but on c-sizing-landscape.jpg (a different image with different content). Asserts cx and cy are between 0 and 1. Ensures the algorithm works across different content types, not just the POI-specific fixture.

column3: POI image brightness centroid is in the top-left quadrant
column4: cx < 0.6 and cy < 0.6
column5: Computes the brightness centroid of c-poi-test.jpg (same method as POI-02) and asserts cx < 0.6 and cy < 0.6. The test image has its subject (a bright region) placed in the top-left area, so the centroid should be biased toward that quadrant. Catches crop logic that would center on the wrong region.

column3: POI image center region is not blank
column4: Center 20x20 region mean luminance between 5 and 250
column5: Downsamples c-poi-test.jpg to 100x100 greyscale, then samples the center 20x20 pixel region (rows 40–59, columns 40–59). Computes mean luminance of that region and asserts it's between 5 and 250. Ensures the image has actual content in the center — a completely black or white center would make centroid tests meaningless.

column3: Candidate longest edge does not exceed resolution cap
column4: Longest edge ≤ TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX (skipped if not set)
column5: Reads c-resolution-cap-test.jpg dimensions from sharp, computes the longest edge (max of width and height), and asserts it's ≤ TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX. Skipped entirely if the env var is not set. When active, enforces the maximum display resolution to catch images that bypass the downscaling pipeline.

column3: Resolution chart longest edge does not exceed resolution cap
column4: Longest edge ≤ TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX (skipped if not set)
column5: Same longest-edge check as RC-01 but on quality-resolution-chart.jpg. Tests a different image type (fine-line resolution chart vs photographic content) that might take a different code path through the resize pipeline. Also skipped if TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX is not set.

column3: All test images have valid dimensions within sane bounds
column4: Every image: width > 0, height > 0, longest edge ≤ 15000
column5: Scans every image file in TEST_IMAGES_DIR (filtering by common image extensions), opens each with sharp, and asserts width > 0, height > 0, and longest edge ≤ 15000. A bulk sanity check that catches accidentally huge or zero-dimension images that would cause confusing failures or timeouts in other tests.

column3: quality-resolution-chart.jpg has valid dimensions
column4: Positive dimensions, longest edge ≤ 15000
column5: Opens quality-resolution-chart.jpg with sharp and asserts width > 0, height > 0, and longest edge ≤ 15000. Validates the resolution chart fixture specifically since it's used for fine-detail sharpness analysis in IQ tests.

column3: Watermark test image loads and is visible in browser
column4: Loads in browser with naturalWidth > 0
column5: Loads c-sizing-small.jpg as a base64 data URL in a Playwright browser page, waits for the image to fully load, and asserts naturalWidth > 0. A basic browser rendering check using the small fixture (safe data URL size) to ensure the test infrastructure works before running pixel analysis.

column3: Watermark test image has valid dimensions
column4: Width and height > 0
column5: Opens c-watermark-test.jpg with sharp and asserts width > 0 and height > 0. A smoke test for the watermark fixture — catches file corruption before running the more expensive uniform block analysis.

column3: Watermark test image has no large uniform rectangular region
column4: Fewer than 95/100 10x10 blocks have variance < 2
column5: Downsamples c-watermark-test.jpg to 100x100 greyscale, divides it into a 10x10 grid of 10x10-pixel blocks, and computes the pixel variance within each block. Counts how many blocks have variance < 2 (nearly uniform). Asserts fewer than 95 out of 100 blocks are uniform. Watermark overlays typically appear as uniform rectangular regions stamped onto the image.

column3: Watermark test image has sufficient tonal variation
column4: Tonal range (max - min luminance) > 20
column5: Downsamples c-watermark-test.jpg to 200x200 greyscale and scans all pixels to find the minimum and maximum luminance values. Computes the tonal range (max - min) and asserts it exceeds 20. Ensures the image has enough contrast for watermark detection to be meaningful — a completely flat image would make pixel-diff watermark tests useless.

column3: Orientation test images have no uniform watermark blocks
column4: Each image has < 20 uniform blocks
column5: Scans TEST_IMAGES_DIR for Landscape_{1-8} and Portrait_{1-8} files. For each, performs the same 10x10 block variance analysis as WM-03 and asserts fewer than 20 blocks are uniform. Checks that orientation processing doesn't accidentally introduce watermark-like uniform rectangular artifacts across all 16 orientation variants.

column3: JPEG quality preserved at each CDN size tier
column4: Uploads JPEG and computes SSIM per tier against locally resized version
column5: Uploads quality-detail.jpg to the test album via the SmugMug Upload API. Queries !sizedetails to get all CDN size tier URLs and dimensions. For each non-original tier, downloads the served image and also creates a locally resized version of the source at the same dimensions using sharp. Computes SSIM between the two. Fails if any tier drops below 0.90, indicating the SmugMug resize/re-encode pipeline introduced visible quality degradation.

column3: Original download matches uploaded file
column4: Compares MD5 of ArchivedUri download against source file
column5: After uploading quality-detail.jpg, fetches the image record from the API to get ArchivedUri and ArchivedMD5. Computes MD5 (base64) of the original source file and asserts it matches ArchivedMD5. Then downloads the file from ArchivedUri and computes MD5 of the downloaded bytes, asserting it also matches. Ensures the original is stored bit-for-bit without any modification.

column3: Original download preserves file size
column4: Compares ArchivedSize API field against source file byte count
column5: After uploading quality-detail.jpg, reads the ArchivedSize field from the API image record and compares it against the source file's byte count from fs.statSync. Asserts exact match. Catches silent re-encoding of originals that would change file size even if visual quality appears preserved.

column3: No double-compression on JPEG uploads
column4: Fetches largest non-original tier and compares SSIM against locally resized Q95
column5: After uploading quality-detail.jpg, finds the largest non-original size tier from !sizedetails (L or XL). Downloads that tier. Also creates a local reference by resizing the source to the same dimensions and encoding as JPEG Q95 using sharp. Computes SSIM between the SmugMug tier and the local reference. Fails below 0.90, which would indicate double-compression artifacts from re-encoding an already-compressed JPEG.

column3: PNG served losslessly at original size
column4: Downloads ArchivedUri and verifies pixel-perfect MD5 match
column5: Uploads quality-reference.png to the test album. Fetches the image record to get ArchivedUri. Downloads the archived file and computes MD5 hex hash. Compares against MD5 of the original source file. Asserts exact match, ensuring PNG originals are stored without any lossy conversion or re-encoding.

column3: PNG resized tiers convert to JPEG acceptably
column4: Fetches M and L tiers of uploaded PNG and compares SSIM
column5: After uploading quality-reference.png, queries !sizedetails and filters for M and L tiers. For each, downloads the served image (which SmugMug converts from PNG to JPEG for resized tiers). Creates a local reference by resizing the source PNG to the same dimensions. Computes SSIM between the SmugMug JPEG tier and the local reference. Fails below 0.90.

column3: GIF upload preserves original
column4: Uploads GIF and verifies ArchivedUri MD5 matches source
column5: Uploads quality-reference.gif to the test album. Fetches the image record to get ArchivedUri. Downloads the archived file and computes MD5 hex hash. Compares against MD5 of the source GIF. Asserts exact match. Skipped if the GIF file doesn't exist in TEST_IMAGES_DIR.

column3: HEIC upload produces viewable JPEG conversion
column4: Uploads HEIC and verifies L/XL tier is valid JPEG with positive dimensions
column5: Uploads quality-reference.heic to the test album. Queries !sizedetails and finds the L or XL tier. Downloads that tier and checks the first two bytes are 0xFF 0xD8 (JPEG magic number). Opens with sharp and asserts width > 0 and height > 0. Validates SmugMug's HEIC-to-JPEG conversion pipeline produces a viewable result. Skipped if the HEIC file doesn't exist.

column3: High-ISO image doesn't gain artifacts after resize
column4: Uploads noisy image and compares L tier SSIM against locally resized version
column5: Uploads quality-noisy.jpg to the test album. Finds the L tier from !sizedetails. Downloads it and also creates a locally resized version of the source at the same dimensions. Computes SSIM between them. Fails below 0.90, catching sharpening halos or noise amplification that SmugMug's resize pipeline might introduce on noisy images.

column3: Image sharpness maintained after resize
column4: Uploads resolution chart and measures Laplacian variance on M and L tiers
column5: Uploads quality-resolution-chart.jpg (containing fine lines and high-contrast patterns) to the test album. Finds M and L tiers from !sizedetails. Downloads each, converts to greyscale, applies Laplacian edge detection, and computes variance. Asserts variance > 50 for each tier, catching over-smoothing or loss of fine detail in the resize pipeline.

column3: Each CDN tier serves correct pixel dimensions
column4: Downloads each tier and verifies actual dimensions match !sizedetails
column5: After uploading c-sizing-landscape.jpg, queries !sizedetails to get all tier URLs with their reported Width and Height. For each tier, downloads the actual image and reads dimensions from sharp metadata. Asserts the actual pixel dimensions exactly match the API-reported dimensions. Catches mismatches between what the API claims and what's actually served.

column3: Aspect ratio preserved across all tiers
column4: Compares each tier's aspect ratio against OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight
column5: Computes the source aspect ratio from OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight on the API image record. For each non-original tier from !sizedetails, computes the tier's aspect ratio from its Width/Height. Asserts the difference is ≤ 0.02. Detects cropping, padding, or stretching introduced during resize.

column3: Landscape image longest edge matches tier spec
column4: Verifies each tier's longest edge is positive for 6000x4000 upload
column5: Queries !sizedetails for the uploaded 6000x4000 landscape image. For each non-original tier, computes the longest edge and asserts it's positive. Validates SmugMug's tier sizing logic generates reasonable dimensions for standard landscape images.

column3: Portrait image longest edge matches tier spec
column4: Uploads 4000x6000 portrait, verifies height >= width at every tier
column5: Uploads c-sizing-portrait.jpg (4000x6000). Queries !sizedetails. For each non-original tier, asserts height >= width. Catches orientation-unaware resize logic that treats all images as landscape and produces landscape-oriented tiers for portrait uploads.

column3: Square image both edges match tier spec
column4: Uploads 5000x5000 square, verifies width === height at every tier
column5: Uploads c-sizing-square.jpg (5000x5000). Queries !sizedetails. For each non-original tier, asserts width === height. Catches rounding errors in resize logic that break the 1:1 aspect ratio by even one pixel.

column3: Panoramic image sizing preserves aspect ratio
column4: Uploads 12000x2000 panoramic, checks ratio drift per tier
column5: Uploads c-sizing-panoramic.jpg (12000x2000, ratio 6:1). Computes source ratio from API OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight. For each non-original tier, checks the tier ratio doesn't drift more than 0.1 from the source. Validates extreme aspect ratios aren't cropped or padded during resize.

column3: Very tall image sizing preserves aspect ratio
column4: Uploads 2000x10000 tall image, checks ratio drift per tier
column5: Uploads c-sizing-tall.jpg (2000x10000, ratio 1:5). Same ratio drift check as SZ-06 but for extreme vertical images. Catches height truncation or padding in the resize pipeline.

column3: Small source not upscaled beyond original
column4: Uploads 400x300 image, verifies no tier exceeds 400x300
column5: Uploads c-sizing-small.jpg (400x300). Queries !sizedetails. For every tier (including larger ones like L and XL), asserts width ≤ 400 and height ≤ 300. Catches upscaling bugs where the CDN generates tiers larger than the source image's native resolution.

column3: !largestimage returns highest available dimensions
column4: Queries !largestimage and verifies valid URL with dimensions ≤ original
column5: Queries both the image record (for OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight) and !largestimage endpoint. Asserts !largestimage returns a valid URL with dimensions that don't exceed the original. Validates the API endpoint that clients use to request the best available resolution.

column3: OriginalWidth/Height match source file
column4: Compares API OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight against sharp metadata of source
column5: Reads the source file's dimensions from sharp metadata. Queries the API image record for OriginalWidth and OriginalHeight. Asserts exact match. Catches dimension reporting bugs in the upload pipeline where the API reports different dimensions than the actual file contains.

column3: OriginalSize matches source file byte count
column4: Compares API OriginalSize against source file byte count
column5: Reads the source file's byte count from fs.statSync. Queries the API image record for OriginalSize. Asserts exact match. Catches file size reporting errors that would break download progress indicators or storage quota calculations.

column3: Orientation N served image is corrected
column4: Uploads all 8 orientation variants, fetches L/XL tier, verifies valid dimensions
column5: Uploads all 8 landscape orientation variants (tags 1–8) to the test album. For each, queries !sizedetails and finds the L or XL tier. Downloads the tier image and opens with sharp to verify it has positive dimensions. Confirms SmugMug's pipeline processes all 8 EXIF orientation tags without producing corrupt or zero-dimension output.

column3: Orientation 6 updates API dimensions to display-corrected
column4: Uploads 6000x4000 with orientation 6, checks OriginalWidth ≤ OriginalHeight
column5: Uploads the 6000x4000 orientation-6 image (raw pixels are landscape, but EXIF tag 6 means it should display as portrait). Queries the API image record and asserts OriginalWidth ≤ OriginalHeight. Validates that SmugMug reports display-corrected dimensions (4000x6000 portrait) rather than raw pixel dimensions (6000x4000 landscape).

column3: Orientation 6 is portrait across all size tiers
column4: Fetches all tiers for orientation-6 image, verifies height >= width
column5: Queries !sizedetails for the orientation-6 image. For each non-original tier, asserts height >= width. Catches tiers that serve the uncorrected landscape orientation — every tier should be portrait after orientation correction.

column3: Orientation 6 renders as portrait in Lightbox
column4: Opens image in Lightbox, checks rendered bounding box is taller than wide
column5: Navigates to the orientation-6 image's WebUri in a Playwright browser. Waits for the first visible img element, reads its bounding box, and asserts height > width. Validates end-to-end orientation correction from upload through CDN through browser rendering.

column3: Orientation 6 thumbnail is portrait in gallery
column4: Navigates to Organize view, verifies thumbnails are present
column5: Navigates to the Organize view for the test album. Waits for thumbnails to load and counts img elements with SmugMug URLs. Asserts at least one thumbnail is present. A best-effort check that the gallery view renders orientation-corrected thumbnails.

column3: Camera make and model preserved
column4: Queries !metadata, verifies Make and Model fields present
column5: Uploads metadata-rich.jpg to the test album. Queries !metadata endpoint. Asserts both Make (or CameraMake) and Model (or CameraModel) fields are truthy. Catches pipelines that strip manufacturer identification during upload processing.

column3: Exposure settings preserved
column4: Verifies ExposureTime, FNumber, ISO present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Asserts ExposureTime (or Exposure), FNumber (or Aperture), and ISO (or ISOSpeedRatings) are all defined. Validates the three core exposure triangle values survive SmugMug's upload pipeline.

column3: Focal length preserved
column4: Verifies FocalLength present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Asserts FocalLength is defined. Ensures lens focal length data survives for photographers who filter or search by lens characteristics.

column3: DateTimeOriginal preserved
column4: Verifies DateTimeOriginal present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Asserts DateTimeOriginal (or DateCreated) is truthy. The most critical metadata field — it drives photo organization, timeline views, sorting, and date-based search throughout SmugMug.

column3: GPS coordinates preserved
column4: Checks Latitude/Longitude on image object and in !metadata
column5: Queries both the image record (for Latitude/Longitude) and !metadata (for GPSLatitude/GPSLongitude). If the source EXIF contains GPS data, asserts at least one endpoint reports non-zero coordinates. Validates geotagging data survives for map and location features.

column3: GPS matches between image and metadata endpoints
column4: Cross-checks GPS coordinates between image object and !metadata
column5: Queries both the image record and !metadata for GPS coordinates. If the image record has non-zero Latitude, checks that !metadata reports a matching value within 0.01 degrees. Catches inconsistencies between two API endpoints that should agree on the same GPS data.

column3: Lens info preserved
column4: Verifies LensModel or LensInfo present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Checks for LensModel, LensInfo, or Lens fields. If the source EXIF contains lens data, asserts at least one of these fields is truthy. Lens data is important for photographers comparing equipment performance.

column3: White balance preserved
column4: Verifies WhiteBalance present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. If the source EXIF contains a WhiteBalance value, asserts the API also returns it. Lower priority field but still expected to survive processing.

column3: Flash status preserved
column4: Verifies Flash value present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. If the source EXIF contains a Flash value, asserts the API also returns it. Flash data helps photographers understand lighting conditions for each shot.

column3: Copyright field preserved
column4: Verifies Copyright present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. If the source EXIF contains a Copyright string, asserts the API returns it. Critical for professional photographers — copyright must never be stripped during upload processing.

column3: Artist/Author field preserved
column4: Verifies Artist or Author present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. If the source EXIF contains an Artist field, asserts the API returns Artist or Author. Attribution data that photographers embed in their files for credit purposes.

column3: IPTC caption preserved as Caption
column4: Uploads metadata-iptc.jpg, verifies Caption field populated
column5: Uploads metadata-iptc.jpg (which has IPTC caption data embedded). Queries both the image record (for Caption) and !metadata (for Caption or Caption-Abstract). Asserts the caption field is populated with a non-empty value. Validates IPTC caption data maps correctly to SmugMug's Caption field.

column3: IPTC keywords preserved as Keywords
column4: Verifies KeywordArray populated with keywords from IPTC data
column5: Queries the image record for the uploaded metadata-iptc.jpg. Reads the KeywordArray field and asserts it's defined and contains at least one keyword. Keywords drive search and organization features — they must survive the upload pipeline.

column3: UserComment EXIF field preserved
column4: Verifies UserComment present in !metadata
column5: Queries !metadata for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. If the source EXIF contains a UserComment field, asserts the API returns it. A free-text field some photographers use for notes or processing history.

column3: Lightbox info panel shows camera make/model
column4: Opens Lightbox, presses i, checks page content for camera brand names
column5: Navigates to the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg's WebUri. Presses 'i' to open the Lightbox info panel. Reads the full page text content and checks for common camera brand names (Canon, Nikon, Sony, Apple, FUJIFILM). A soft check since page structure varies across SmugMug themes.

column3: Lightbox info panel shows exposure settings
column4: Checks page content for f-stop, ISO, shutter speed patterns
column5: Opens the Lightbox info panel for metadata-rich.jpg. Scans page text for exposure-related patterns: f/ followed by a digit (aperture), ISO followed by digits, or 1/ followed by digits (shutter speed). Validates exposure data is formatted and displayed.

column3: Lightbox info panel shows focal length
column4: Checks page content for mm focal length pattern
column5: Opens the Lightbox info panel for metadata-rich.jpg. Scans page text for a number followed by 'mm' (case-insensitive). Validates focal length is displayed in the info panel.

column3: Lightbox info panel shows date taken
column4: Checks page content for date patterns
column5: Opens the Lightbox info panel for metadata-rich.jpg. Scans page text for a 4-digit year and a month name or numeric date pattern. Validates the capture date is displayed.

column3: Lightbox shows GPS when Geography enabled
column4: Checks page content for coordinates or location text
column5: Opens the Lightbox info panel for metadata-rich.jpg. Scans page text for decimal coordinate patterns, 'Map', or 'Location' text. Validates location data appears when the gallery has Geography enabled.

column3: Lightbox hides GPS when Geography disabled
column4: Skipped — requires gallery setting toggle
column5: Skipped — requires toggling the gallery's Geography setting via the SmugMug admin UI, which is not automated in this test suite. Would verify no location data appears in the info panel after disabling Geography.

column3: Lightbox hides GPS when site-level GPS off
column4: Skipped — requires site setting toggle
column5: Skipped — requires toggling the site-level GPS privacy setting in Site Settings, which is not automated. Would verify no location data appears after disabling GPS at the site level.

column3: EXIF hidden when gallery EXIF setting is off
column4: Skipped — requires gallery setting toggle
column5: Skipped — requires toggling the gallery's EXIF display setting, which is not automated. Would verify the Lightbox info panel hides all EXIF fields when the setting is off.

column3: Stripped image info panel has no undefined/null text
column4: Opens Lightbox for stripped image, checks body for undefined/null
column5: Navigates to the uploaded metadata-stripped.jpg's WebUri. Presses 'i' to open the info panel. Reads the full page text and asserts it does not contain the literal strings 'undefined' or 'null'. Catches broken template rendering when EXIF data is completely absent.

column3: !metadata returns key EXIF fields
column4: Queries !metadata, checks for Make/Model/ExposureTime/FNumber/ISO/FocalLength/DateTimeOriginal
column5: Uploads metadata-rich.jpg and queries !metadata. Checks for the presence of Make, Model, ExposureTime, FNumber, ISO, FocalLength, and DateTimeOriginal (including common variant key names). Counts how many are found and asserts at least 5 of 7 are present. Validates the API returns the expected superset of camera EXIF fields.

column3: !metadata for stripped image returns without error
column4: Queries !metadata for stripped image, verifies no camera fields and no error
column5: Uploads metadata-stripped.jpg and queries !metadata. Asserts the response is defined (no HTTP error). Then checks that camera fields (Make, Model, ExposureTime, FNumber) are all absent. Ensures the API handles missing metadata gracefully rather than erroring or inventing default values.

column3: Format field matches uploaded file format
column4: Checks Format field on image object contains jpg
column5: Queries the image record for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Reads the Format field and asserts it contains 'jpg' (case-insensitive). Validates format detection in the upload pipeline — a wrong format would cause incorrect content-type headers.

column3: FileName preserves original filename
column4: Checks FileName field matches metadata-rich.jpg
column5: Queries the image record for the uploaded metadata-rich.jpg. Reads the FileName field and asserts it exactly equals 'metadata-rich.jpg'. Catches filename mangling during upload where the pipeline might rename, sanitize, or truncate the original filename.

column3: KeywordArray round-trips correctly
column4: PATCHes keywords, GETs image, verifies all keywords returned
column5: PATCHes the image record with KeywordArray: ['sunset', 'ocean', 'HDR']. Then GETs the image record and reads KeywordArray. Asserts all three keywords are present in the returned array. Validates the keyword write/read cycle through the API — keywords drive search and organization.

column3: Title/Caption round-trip with special characters
column4: PATCHes title/caption with unicode/emoji/special chars, verifies exact match
column5: PATCHes the image record with a title containing em-dashes, accented characters, and emoji (🌅), and a caption containing umlauts, smart quotes, and Japanese characters. GETs the image record and asserts both Title and Caption exactly match what was sent. Catches encoding bugs that mangle non-ASCII text.

column3: !regions returns face/object regions for XMP image
column4: Uploads XMP regions image, queries !regions, verifies regions returned
column5: Uploads c-metadata-xmp-regions.jpg (which has XMP face region data embedded). Queries !regions endpoint. Asserts at least one region is returned. Validates that SmugMug parses XMP face/object region data and makes it accessible through the API. Skipped if the XMP regions file doesn't exist.

column3: Default POI is near geometric center
column4: Queries !pointofinterest before setting one, checks coordinates within bounds
column5: Uploads c-poi-test.jpg and queries !pointofinterest before setting any POI. If a default POI is returned, asserts x and y are both between 0 and 1. Validates the default crop center behavior — images with no explicit POI should either return null or center coordinates.

column3: Setting POI updates crop center
column4: Sets POI to (0.25, 0.25) via API, reads back, verifies match
column5: Sets the Point of Interest to (0.25, 0.25) via PATCH to !pointofinterest. Then GETs !pointofinterest and asserts the returned x is close to 0.25 and y is close to 0.25 (within 0.1 tolerance). Validates the POI write/read round-trip through the API.

column3: POI behavior after image replace
column4: Sets POI, replaces image via upload API, checks persistence
column5: Sets POI to (0.75, 0.75), then replaces the image by uploading c-sizing-landscape.jpg with the replaceImageUri option pointing to the original upload. Queries !pointofinterest after replacement and logs whether the POI persisted or reset. Asserts coordinates are within valid bounds if present. Documents the platform's POI persistence behavior.

column3: POI coordinates round-trip correctly
column4: Sets POI to (0.33, 0.67), reads back, verifies within tolerance
column5: Sets POI to (0.33, 0.67) via PATCH. GETs !pointofinterest and asserts x is close to 0.33 and y is close to 0.67 (within 0.1 tolerance). Tests a different coordinate pair than POI-02 to validate precision across the coordinate space.

column3: POI affects all cropped size tiers
column4: Sets POI, queries !sizedetails, verifies all tiers generated
column5: Sets POI to (0.25, 0.25), then queries !sizedetails. Asserts at least one tier is returned and every tier has positive width and height. Confirms that setting a POI doesn't break tier generation — all tiers should still be produced normally.

column3: Watermark present on visitor-facing tiers
column4: Uploads to watermark-enabled gallery, fetches all tiers, verifies valid images
column5: Uploads c-watermark-test.jpg to the test album. Queries the image record to check the Watermark boolean flag. Queries !sizedetails and downloads every tier, asserting each has non-zero byte length. Validates watermarked tiers are generated and downloadable. Actual watermark pixel verification depends on gallery watermark settings being enabled.

column3: Owner download has no watermark (MD5 matches source)
column4: Downloads ArchivedUri as owner and compares MD5 against source
column5: Downloads the archived original from ArchivedUri (as the authenticated owner). Computes MD5 hex hash of the download and compares against MD5 of the source file. Asserts exact match. The owner's archived download should never contain a watermark regardless of gallery settings.

column3: Archived original has no watermark
column4: Checks ArchivedSize matches source file byte count
column5: Reads ArchivedSize from the API image record and compares against the source file's byte count from fs.statSync. Asserts exact match. A complementary check to WM-02 — if the archived file has a watermark baked in, its size would differ from the source.

column3: Watermark pixel diff between tiers is consistent
column4: Downloads small and large tiers, verifies both are valid images
column5: Downloads both a small tier (Th or S) and a large tier (L or XL) from !sizedetails. Opens each with sharp and asserts positive dimensions. Validates that watermark rendering (if enabled) doesn't corrupt or break images at different tier sizes.

column3: All tiers are valid images (watermark scaling check)
column4: Downloads every tier, verifies actual dimensions match API-reported dimensions
column5: Downloads every tier from !sizedetails. For each, opens with sharp and asserts the actual pixel dimensions match the API-reported Width and Height. Catches watermark rendering bugs that might corrupt specific tier sizes or produce dimension mismatches.

column3: No tier exceeds resolution cap
column4: Fetches all tiers, verifies no non-original tier exceeds TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX
column5: Uploads c-resolution-cap-test.jpg. Queries !sizedetails. For each non-original tier, computes the longest edge and asserts it's ≤ TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX. Skipped if the env var is not set. Enforces the display resolution cap across all CDN tiers for visitor-facing images.

column3: Owner can access full resolution tiers
column4: Queries !largestimage as owner, verifies full resolution accessible
column5: Queries both the image record (for OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight) and !largestimage endpoint as the authenticated owner. Asserts !largestimage returns a valid URL with positive dimensions. Ensures the resolution cap doesn't affect the owner's access to their own full-resolution images.

column3: Owner archived download is full resolution
column4: Downloads ArchivedUri, verifies dimensions match source file
column5: Reads the source file's dimensions from sharp metadata. Queries the API image record for OriginalWidth/OriginalHeight and asserts they match the source. Downloads ArchivedUri and reads dimensions from the downloaded buffer. Asserts those also match. Confirms the resolution cap doesn't affect the stored original.

column3: Lightbox image respects resolution cap
column4: Opens Lightbox, inspects loaded image dimensions against cap
column5: Navigates to the image's WebUri in a Playwright browser. Waits for network idle. Evaluates all img elements in the DOM to find one with naturalWidth > 100. Reads its dimensions and asserts the longest edge is ≤ TEST_RESOLUTION_CAP_MAX. Skipped if the env var is not set. Validates the cap is enforced in the browser viewer, not just at the API level.

column3: HEIC with text overlay converts to viewable JPEG tiers
column4: Uploads HEIC, fetches L/XL tier, verifies valid JPEG with positive dimensions
column5: Uploads text-over-image.heic (a portrait iPhone photo with text overlaid) to the test album. Queries !sizedetails and finds the L or XL tier. Downloads it and checks the first two bytes are 0xFF 0xD8 (JPEG magic). Opens with sharp and asserts positive dimensions. Validates SmugMug's HEIC conversion handles images with sharp text edges without producing corrupt output.

column3: Converted image preserves portrait orientation
column4: Checks OriginalHeight > OriginalWidth on API image object
column5: Queries the API image record for the uploaded text-over-image.heic. Asserts OriginalHeight > OriginalWidth. The source is 3024x4032 (portrait) — this catches orientation bugs where a portrait HEIC gets converted to landscape during the HEIC-to-JPEG conversion pipeline.

column3: All size tiers maintain portrait aspect ratio
column4: Fetches all tiers, verifies height >= width at every tier
column5: Queries !sizedetails for the uploaded HEIC. For each non-original tier, asserts height >= width. Ensures the portrait orientation is preserved across the full CDN tier stack, not just the original — every served size should be portrait.

column3: Text sharpness preserved in converted L tier
column4: Downloads L tier and measures Laplacian variance (must be > 50)
column5: Downloads the L or XL tier of the converted HEIC. Converts to greyscale with sharp, applies Laplacian edge detection across all interior pixels, and computes variance. Asserts variance > 50. Text overlays produce high-contrast edges — low variance would mean the HEIC-to-JPEG conversion blurred or smeared the text.

column3: Archived original matches source file
column4: Compares ArchivedSize and downloaded byte count against source
column5: Reads ArchivedSize from the API image record and compares against the source HEIC file's byte count. Downloads ArchivedUri and checks the downloaded byte count also matches. Ensures the original HEIC is stored unmodified even though served tiers are converted to JPEG.
